principles.name: Reciprocity
principles.description: People feel obliged to give back to others who have given to them.
principles.focus: Does the site offer value upfront (e.g., free guides, templates, valuable content) before asking for anything (email, purchase)? Is the 'ask' balanced with the 'give'?

principles.name: Commitment & Consistency
principles.description: Once people make a choice/take a stand, they encounter personal and interpersonal pressures to behave consistently with that commitment.
principles.focus: Does the site ask for small initial commitments (e.g., 'Read more', 'Sign up for newsletter') that lead to larger ones? Are there opportunities for users to publicly support or engage?

principles.name: Social Proof
principles.description: People look to others to determine what is correct, especially when they are uncertain.
principles.focus: Are there testimonials, reviews, case studies, subscriber counts, or 'As seen in' logos? Is the social proof specific and credible?

principles.name: Authority
principles.description: People respect authority and want to follow the lead of real experts.
principles.focus: Does the content demonstrate expertise? Are author credentials, awards, certifications, or partnerships visible? Is the tone authoritative and confident?

principles.name: Liking
principles.description: People prefer to say yes to those that they like.
principles.focus: Is the brand voice relatable and human? Is the 'About Us' page engaging? excellent design and aesthetics? Do the images show real people?

principles.name: Scarcity
principles.description: The thought of losing something motivates people more than the thought of gaining something of equal value.
principles.focus: Are there limited-time offers, exclusive content, or indications of high demand? Is scarcity used ethically to encourage action?
